channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This ultra-lightweight portable 15.6-inch monitor and magnetic stand could be the ideal productivity accessory for professionals on the go
item.description: This ultra-lightweight 15.6-inch portable display and magnetic stand could significantly boost your productivity.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: The best cozy games during a stressful Thanksgiving season
item.description: For many, video games are loud, violent, and competitive. But for some they are the opposite: quiet, soft, and low-stakes. Welcome to the world of cozy gaming.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: The lingering questions around Nvidia’s strong quarterly report
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Technology Letter and is republished here with permission. Late Wednesday evening, some men were sitting around the Russian baths in Wall Street’s financial district talking about Nvidia.
item.dc:creator: Tiernan Ray, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk attacks Australian government over proposed social media ban
item.description: An Australian Cabinet minister on Friday rejected X Corp. owner Elon Musk’s allegation that the government intended to control all Australians’ access to the internet through legislation that would ban young children from social media.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: UK regulator says Apple, Google blocking innovation in mobile browsers
item.description: The United Kingdom’s top antitrust regulator said Friday that an independent inquiry provisionally found that Apple's and Google’s policies for mobile browsers are blocking innovation.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Bitcoin ticks closer to $100,000 in extended surge following US elections
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin extended its streak of record highs after ticking above $99,000 for the first time. The cryptocurrency has rocketed more than 40% in just two weeks.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: TypeScript 5.7 arrives with improved error reporting
item.description: TypeScript 5.7, the latest planned release of Microsoft’s JavaScript variant with syntax for types, is now available in a production release. With version 5.7, the language’s type system is now able to report errors when variables have never been initialized.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: In the wake of Java Development Kit (JDK) 23, which arrived September 17, work now focuses on the planned successor release, JDK 24, which has ballooned to 24 proposed features. The four most recent additions include warnings upon the use of memory-access methods in sun.misc.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Bluesky Verification: A Need to Prioritize Trust
item.description: Importance of verification on social media platforms like Bluesky, addressing fake accounts and establishing credibility.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Supreme Court allows multibillion-dollar class action to proceed against Meta
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court is allowing a multibillion-dollar class action investors’ lawsuit to proceed against Facebook parent Meta, stemming from the privacy scandal involving the Cambridge Analytica political consulting firm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The future according to Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis
item.description: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis used to get some of his most fulfilling work done between midnight and 3 a.m. A self-described “nocturnal person,” the Londoner devoted the solitude of his overnight shift to catching up on scientific papers, coming up with new ideas, and just plain thinking.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI is thinking about building its own browser
item.description: OpenAI is reportedly thinking about developing its own browser with the aim of challenging Google’s dominance in the market, according to The Information. The new browser would have built-in support for Chat GPT and Open AI’s search engine Search GPT.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Consumer watchdog to oversee digital wallets, payment apps
item.description: Large companies that offer digital wallets and payment apps will now be overseen by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB). The CFPB finalized a rule Thursday to bring companies handling more than 50 million transactions a year under its supervision.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Apple Pay, other tech firms come under CFPB regulatory oversight
item.description: CFPB can already take action against companies that break the law, the new rule would allow the CFPB to regularly supervise the large digital-wallet and payments firms and their practices.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: AI networking a focus of HPE’s Juniper deal as Justice Department concerns swirl
item.description: Amid reports of US regulatory concerns over HPE’s proposed buy of Juniper Networks, the CEOs of both companies talked about the future of the blended entity at this week’s HPE Discover event in Barcelona – and that future includes plenty of AI networking technology.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A blueprint for successfully executing business-aligned IT strategies
item.description: In our fast-changing digital world, it’s essential to sync IT strategies with business objectives for lasting success. Technology has shifted from a back-office function to a core enabler of business growth, innovation, and competitive advantage.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: A Thai court has dismissed a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist who alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: Jerry Harmer And David Rising, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: AI-powered dog robot sniffs out invasive fire ants
item.description: Fueled by artificial intelligence, CyberDog the robotic dog is designed specifically locate fire ant nests, which are endangering ecosystems.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iceland's Sleeping Giant Roars Back to Life: Seventh Eruption in Three Months - A Volcanic Crisis Unfolds
item.description: Iceland's Meradalir volcano erupts for the seventh time since December, sending molten lava towards the Blue Lagoon spa and threatening nearby towns.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Nvidia is Wall Street's most valuable company. How it got there, by the numbers
item.description: Nvidia has once again turned out quarterly results that exceeded Wall Street's forecasts. The company has seen soaring demand for its semiconductors, which are used to power artificial intelligence applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Comcast to spin off cable networks that were once the entertainment giant's star performers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Comcast is spinning off into a new company many of the cable television networks that were once at the entertainment giant's heart as consumers increasingly swap out their cable TV subscriptions for streaming platforms.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Microsoft Ignite 2024 — get the latest news and insights
item.description: Microsoft Ignite 2024 kicks off in Chicago and runs Nov. 19-22. If you can’t make it to Chicago, no worries. First, the physical event is sold out, according to the Ignite event page. Second, it’s a hybrid event, so you can attend Ignite virtually.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: EU industry group has its eye on Microsoft’s cloud business
item.description: Microsoft’s cloud business is under surveillance in Europe: The association of Cloud Infrastructure Services Providers in Europe (CISPE) has set up new monitoring body to ensure the tech giant complies with a legal settlement to keep cloud licensing practices fair in the region.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: NetOps startup Selector AI wants to solve the network noise problem
item.description: Back in 2019, Kannan Kothandaraman helped to start Selector AI after spending years in the networking trenches at big vendors including Juniper and Cisco.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: US commission proposes ‘Manhattan Project-like’ initiative for AI
item.description: A US congressional commission has called for a “Manhattan Project-like” initiative to accelerate artificial intelligence (AI) development, urging Congress to grant the executive branch sweeping, multiyear contracting authority to fund advancements in AI, cloud computing, and data centers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to "act on our behalf across our work and life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A matter of time: 3 metrics that demonstrate the value of immersion cooling
item.description: Immersion cooling in the data center space gets a lot of positive attention for the formulations that enhance thermal performance, material compatibility, and durability.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: This tool is made for the laziest parent ever
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how an innovative Easy-Way kit turns standard strollers electric, simplifying navigation for parents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to transform your architecture review board
item.description: I recall my angst about my first visit to an architecture review board (ARB), where a team of enterprise architects reviewed plans for new systems and application architectures.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Company affiliated with Alex Jones seeks to disqualify The Onion's auction bid on Infowars
item.description: A company affiliated with conspiracy theorist Alex Jones asked a federal judge on Monday to disqualify a bid by the satirical news outlet The Onion to buy Jones' Infowars at a bankruptcy auction, alleging fraud and collusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge tosses Huckabee lawsuit against Meta over ads suggesting he endorsed marijuana gummies
item.description: WILMINGTON, Del. (AP) — A federal judge in Delaware on Monday dismissed a lawsuit filed by former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee against social media giant Meta over advertisements using his name and image to sell CBD products.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Chinese humanoid robot shows off its strength by lifting 35 pounds per hand
item.description: China's Shanghai Kepler Robotics has developed a new humanoid robot that can carry up to 35 pounds per hand for commercial applications across various industries
item.dc:creator: Fox News
